item.title: Harvard professor known for alien theories to lead White House UFO council
item.description: Avi Loeb, a Harvard astronomer who has made headlines with his theories about alien encounters, will lead a White House scientific advisory council on UFOs.

item.title: Why U.S. initially restricted Anthropic's advanced AI models
item.description: The Trump administration has lifted export restrictions on Anthropic's most advanced artificial intelligence models, Fable and Mythos, after weeks of negotiations over national security concerns. CNET Editor-in-Chief David Katzmaier joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump responds to criticism of his crypto earnings: "Everybody's profiting"
item.description: President Trump responded to critics on Wednesday who say he's profiting off the presidency after a new financial disclosure showed he made over $1 billion on cryptocurrency ventures in 2025. Victor Reklaitis, Washington correspondent for MarketWatch, joins with analysis.

item.title: House lawmakers start July 4th recess early after some GOP reps block bill merger
item.description: House lawmakers began their July 4th recess early on Tuesday after some GOP members blocked Speaker Mike Johnson from merging the SAVE America Act with the annual defense policy bill, known as the National Defense Authorization Act. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: U.S. says it won't extend key trade deal with Canada and Mexico
item.description: The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement will remain in effect until it expires in 2036, unless the countries strike another deal to extend it.

item.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers in custody after apparently getting engaged at the top
item.description: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Couples are spending 8.5% more on weddings this year. Here's why.
item.description: Tariffs, inflation and changing consumer habits are reshaping how much Americans spend to tie the knot.

item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angelina Nikolau, 33, law enforcement sources told CBS News.

item.title: DOJ investigating Sen. Ruben Gallego's use of campaign funds, sources say
item.description: The probe focuses on Gallego's use of campaign funds for family trips, a source told CBS News. He has denied wrongdoing.

item.title: What is the "buy, borrow, die" tax strategy Gavin Newsom wants to ban?
item.description: As a proposed billionaire tax in California moves forward, Gov. Newsom says other approaches are needed, including closing a tax loophole used by the ultra-rich.

item.title: South Carolina woman on her values, story and Southern accent as America celebrates 250 years
item.description: For America's 250th anniversary, CBS News spoke to Karen Norris Newsome, a 74-year-old woman from Darlington County, South Carolina, about her childhood, her values and her quintessential Southern accent.

item.title: Why a CD account is worth opening this July
item.description: A CD account has been especially worth opening in recent years, but especially so this July. Here's why.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: What we know about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's rumored MSG wedding
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding festivities are expected to begin Thursday, sources said. Crews have been spotted at Madison Square Garden in New York City unloading equipment ahead of the July Fourth weekend. Here's what we know so far.

item.title: How much interest can a $55,000 CD account earn if opened now?
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account of this size will be substantial. Here's what savers need to know right now.

item.title: Former CIA Director John Brennan sues DOJ, Trump officials over criminal probes
item.description: Brennan is seeking a court order to force the preservation of records in the investigations targeting him. His attorneys say he's being "vindictively singled out for investigation."

item.title: USMNT starts World Cup knockout stage against Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team will play their first knockout stage match of the 2026 World Cup Wednesday night against Bosnia and Herzegovina. CBS News' Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: U.S. helicopter goes down in Arabian Sea, crew member missing, Navy says
item.description: Three members of a four-person U.S. helicopter crew were rescued at sea after an "emergency water landing" in the Arabian Sea on Wednesday, the Navy said. A search continues for the fourth and final crew member.

item.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
item.description: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

item.title: Melat Kiros projected to defeat Diana DeGette in Colorado Congressional primary
item.description: Challenger Melat Kiros, a democratic socialist, is the projected winner of the Democratic primary in Colorado's First Congressional District.

item.title: U.S. helicopter goes down in Arabian Sea, crew member missing, Navy says
item.description: The helicopter, which was assigned to the USS George H.W. Bush, is not believed to have been taken down by hostile action, the Navy said.

item.title: More than 1,000 cases under review after Broadview Six misconduct revelations
item.description: More than 1,000 grand jury presentations are under review after charges were dismissed in the "Broadview Six" case​ due to grand jury abuses and prosecutorial misconduct, Chicago's top federal prosecutor said.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: Here's a look at how events have unfolded in the Karen Read case.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Justice Department sues Virginia, California over gun laws
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche said "the Constitution is not a suggestion" and that "the Second Amendment is a sacred right belonging to all Americans."

item.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

item.title: This number helps explain why many Americans are down on the economy
item.description: American workers' share of the nation's income is at its lowest point in almost 80 years, as more of the economy's gains flow to corporations and investors.

item.title: Anthropic says Trump admin. lifted restrictions on powerful Claude AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence giant Anthropic says the federal government lifted a set of restrictions on its powerful Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, resolving a weekslong dispute.

item.title: Egg producers reach federal and state settlement after price-fixing probe
item.description: Three U.S. egg producers will be required to provide 53 million eggs to food banks and to pay a $3.3 million financial penalty.

item.title: Videos show U.S. rescue team pull mother, baby from rubble in Venezuela
item.description: Hundreds of U.S. search and rescue workers are on the ground in Venezuela after deadly quakes struck the South American nation.
